message:
fix(index): make WAL cleanup best-effort and add temp-file cleanup on rewrite error

After compaction, clear in-memory WAL state immediately once rewrite_index
succeeds, then attempt to remove the WAL file on disk as a best-effort
operation. If remove_wal fails, the stale file is harmless — the generation
counter ensures it will be detected and discarded on the next open(). This
avoids propagating a non-fatal filesystem error as a compaction failure.

In rewrite_index, wrap the I/O block in a closure so that any failure after
the temp file is created triggers best-effort cleanup of the temp file before
returning the error, preventing orphaned .tmp files from accumulating.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
